title: Break & Continue 🛑
description: Take manual control over how loops operate.

slides.title: Breaking the Cycle 🛑
slides.content:
Sometimes you want an escape hatch to violently exit a loop before the condition naturally becomes False.

The **`break;`** keyword instantly stops the entire loop and jumps out of the `{}` braces entirely.

slides.title: Break Example
slides.content: We planned to count to 100, but we hit an emergency abort at 3.

quizData.question: What happens when a loop encounters the `break;` command?

options.text: It pauses and waits for user input.
It skips the current loop and jumps to the next iteration.
It instantly exits the entire loop and continues executing the code beneath it.

quizData.explanation: `break` shatters the loop immediately, returning execution to the main code below the loop.

slides.title: Skipping to the End ⏭️
slides.content:
What if you don't want to destroy the loop, but you just want to skip the *current* cycle and jump to the next one?

The **`continue;`** keyword skips any code below it and forces the loop back to the top to start the next round.

slides.title: Continue Example
slides.content: Let's print odds but skip the even numbers.

quizData.question: The `continue;` keyword terminates the entire loop, just like `break;`.
quizData.explanation: `continue` only skips the REST of the CURRENT iteration. It jumps back to the top to evaluate the next cycle. `break` stops the loop forever.
